channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: 2026 McKinney Writing Contest is open!
item.description: The 2026 McKinney Writing Contest is open from November 1, 2025 through March 9, 2026. Submit in any or all of the following categories: Fiction, Creative Nonfiction, or Drama; Poetry; Academic Essay; Language & Image; or our NEW category: Writing for Games. Undergraduate and graduate divisions. First prize $300; 2nd prize $175; and 3rd prize $75. Submit: https://hass.rpi.edu/mckinney. Submit in fall for December graduates. Questions? McKinney@rpi.edu
item.author: Students

item.title: Systems Engineering & Technology Management Review with RPI at Work
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 5, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - This 30-minute information session is hosted by Dean Aric Krause, Ph.D., and will focus on RPI’s Systems Engineering & Technology Management graduate degree. The degree is digitally delivered, flexible, and project-based to prepare decision-makers with broader and deeper capabilities in leading dynamic organizations. The session will highlight program details and how you can select graduate certificates relevant to your work to complete…
item.author: Community

item.title: DotCIO requests feedback on the student laptop program and for help selecting next year’s student laptops
item.description: The Mobile Computing Program is asking for feedback from the community to help select the most appropriate laptop models and specifications for this coming year. Additionally, we are seeking general feedback on the program. Please take a few minutes to complete this survey which is open through March 7, 2026.
item.author: Community

item.title: Information Security - PhishStick - Our new AI Phishing Coach is now live!
item.description: After majority vote from our RPI Community, Congratulations to our winner of the Information Security AI Phishing Coach naming contest! We are excited to announce that the name for our AI Phish Coach will be: PhishStick! Our Phishing Coach and email simulation training are now active for our RPI Community. Please click above link for more information.
item.author: Community

item.title: Rensselaer Faulty and Staff In Person Workshop: Active Learning Examples to Keep Your Students Engaged
item.description:
Date: Monday, March 16, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - If you are interested in hearing more about various active learning exercises that you may be able to implement into your course, then this workshop is for you. Please join me to discuss the practice of active learning, the benefits, and some of the more popular active learning exercises. Maureen Fodera (Office of Undergraduate Education) will be presenting this webinar. Please register by 3/11 using the Event URL in the See section of the…

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
